frameworks.name: NeuroAI
frameworks.one_line: neuroscience-grounded research programme positioned to catalyze next-generation AI.

frameworks.name: embodied Turing test
frameworks.one_line: benchmark proposal: AI animal models must match living counterparts in sensorimotor interaction.

frameworks.name: classical Turing test
frameworks.one_line: language- and game-centric evaluation deprecated in favor of embodied skills.

core_question: How should AI be evaluated and advanced so that it captures evolutionarily shared animal capabilities rather than uniquely human ones?
key_assumptions: neuroscience has historically driven progress in AI
sensorimotor capabilities shared across animals reflect 500M years of evolution
embodied animal-level skills are a meaningful benchmark for AI
interpretations: investing in NeuroAI fundamentals will accelerate AI progress
passing the embodied Turing test provides a roadmap for next-generation AI
